# Mainthread

> One Life, One System — a personal productivity platform for high-performance executives who manage complex work and personal commitments across disconnected tools.

Mainthread replaces the fragmentation of juggling multiple life domains (work, family, household) with a single unified system. It is positioned not against productivity apps, but against the human alternatives: executive assistants and chiefs of staff.

## Core Concepts

### Swimlanes
The top-level organizational unit. Each swimlane represents a life domain — for example: Work, Family, Household. Everything lives inside a swimlane.

### Threads
Long-running projects or areas of responsibility within a swimlane. Threads have completion criteria and span days, weeks, or months. Examples: "Close Series A", "Plan family vacation to Japan", "Kitchen renovation".

### Tasks
Individual units of work within a thread. Tasks have deadlines, effort estimates, priority scores, and optional delegation to an AI agent. Tasks are the atomic unit that the system acts on daily.

### Priority Engine
A daily scoring system that ranks tasks across all swimlanes using a weighted combination of: urgency, consequence of inaction, stakeholder impact, thread importance, user flags, and dependency ordering. The engine produces a capacity-aware daily shortlist — the tasks that actually matter today.

## AI Features

### Concierge Agent
A context-aware AI assistant ("smart butler") with full read/write access to all Mainthread data. Accessible via in-app chat and WhatsApp (dedicated phone number). Handles natural language queries, task creation, status updates, and briefings. Memory is persistent across sessions and channels.

Planned proactive behaviors (v1+):
- Daily briefings
- Deadline monitoring
- Smart delegation suggestions
- Ingestion of incoming emails and messages into tasks

### Task Executor Agent
An AI agent that performs real-world administrative tasks on the user's behalf via browser automation (Stagehand + Browserbase). Users delegate tasks; the agent executes them using web browsers — filling forms, booking services, paying bills, and more.

Execution follows an Understand → Plan → Reflect reasoning loop. When the agent requires sensitive input (passwords, credit card numbers) or human judgment, it pauses via a Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) modal and waits for user response before continuing. Sensitive data is never persisted server-side.

### Playbooks & Skills
Users author Skills — free-text instructions for how to perform specific recurring tasks (e.g., "Pay arnona", "Book a Pilates class"). Skills are grouped into Playbooks scoped to the user. When the Task Executor Agent runs a task, relevant Skills are loaded dynamically, giving the agent personalized context and preferences.

### Effort Estimation
At task ingestion, an LLM pipeline estimates effort by decomposing the task into subtasks and assigning confidence-weighted estimates. LLM estimates are blended with user-provided estimates (~70/30, adjusted by confidence). User-provided estimates are treated as unreliable hints, not ground truth.

### Scheduling
An event-driven LLM-powered scheduler assigns start dates to incoming tasks based on current capacity and dependency ordering. A deterministic validator acts as a safety net. The system is not cron-based — scheduling events fire when tasks are created or updated.

## Philosophy

**One Life, One System.** Work and personal life don't have separate operating systems. Mainthread treats them as one portfolio of commitments, prioritized together.

**Delegation, not management.** The product's direction is toward a personal delegation layer — users delegate tasks to AI agents rather than managing them manually. Only check-in time is counted against personal capacity for delegated tasks.

**Vertical, not horizontal.** Every feature ships end-to-end (UI + API + data) in working slices. No half-built layers.

## Target Users

**High-performance executives** who:
- Manage 5–15 active long-running commitments across work and personal life simultaneously
- Are currently served by an executive assistant or chief of staff (or wish they were)
- Are overwhelmed by context-switching between tools, not by lack of tools
- Want AI to act, not just remind

**Owner-operators** who:
- Run small businesses without back-office staff (field trades, service businesses, solo operators)
- Are simultaneously the CEO, admin, and frontline worker
- Need a system that handles mundane operational tasks so they can stay focused on the work itself
- Use Mainthread as their back office — automating scheduling, follow-ups, payments, and coordination
